page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, providing a total of 160 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz, a 38 MB L3 cache, and 340.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25%, and single-threaded compression tasks are similarly limited. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the middle 50%. The server excels in parallelized execution, particularly in large language model inference. Benchmark results place prompt processing speeds for 135M, 7B, and 70B models, as well as text generation for 7B and 70B models, in the top 10% tier.

From a resource density perspective, this server represents a specialized hardware profile where cost efficiency is tied directly to GPU utilization rather than raw CPU compute. The inclusion of four dedicated A100 GPUs makes it highly effective for deep learning, LLM inference, and parallel data science jobs, whereas general-purpose or single-threaded workloads would underutilize the hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB of total VRAM, paired with 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture and 340.0 GB of system memory. While single-core CPU benchmarks place this instance in the bottom 25%, its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth sit in the middle 50%. The server demonstrates top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference, specifically in prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. Lacking local storage, this configuration is designed for GPU-intensive workloads, including deep learning, parallel data science tasks, and LLM deployment, where the high-density accelerator configuration justifies the hardware profile.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 160 GB VRAM, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 340 GB RAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 340 GB RAM, optimized for high-tier LLM inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring four dedicated GPUs and high-capacity memory for demanding parallel computing and large language model inference.

48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 340.0 GB of system memory
